questions.q: Which numbered President of India was A.P.J. Abdul Kalam?
questions.explanation: Dr. A.P.J. Abdul Kalam was the eleventh President of India.

questions.q: In which year did A.P.J. Abdul Kalam begin his term as President of India?
questions.explanation: He assumed office as President in 2002.

questions.q: In which year did A.P.J. Abdul Kalam complete his term as President of India?
questions.explanation: His presidential term concluded in 2007.

questions.q: Before becoming President, A.P.J. Abdul Kalam was renowned in which profession?
questions.explanation: He was a renowned aerospace scientist before becoming President.

questions.q: A.P.J. Abdul Kalam is widely remembered for his work in India's programmes related to which fields?
questions.explanation: He is remembered for his work in India's space and missile programmes.

questions.q: By what popular description is A.P.J. Abdul Kalam often remembered, as reflected on his card?
options: The Father of the Lok Sabha
questions.explanation: His card subtitle describes him as 'The People's President.'

questions.q: The President's office held by Kalam is the constitutional head of what?
questions.explanation: The President of India is the constitutional head of state.

questions.q: Roughly how many years did A.P.J. Abdul Kalam serve as President?
questions.explanation: He served from 2002 to 2007, a term of about five years.

questions.q: What does the academic title 'Dr.' before Kalam's name commonly reflect?
options: Recognition of his scholarly and scientific standing
questions.explanation: The honorific reflects his scholarly and scientific standing as a distinguished scientist.

questions.q: Kalam's term as President fell within which decade?
questions.explanation: His 2002 to 2007 term fell within the 2000s.

questions.q: The President, the office Kalam held, is elected by an electoral college of which members?
options: Elected MPs and members of state legislative assemblies
questions.explanation: The President is elected by an electoral college of elected MPs and members of state legislative assemblies.

questions.q: As President, A.P.J. Abdul Kalam was the supreme commander of which body?
questions.explanation: The President of India is the supreme commander of the armed forces.

questions.q: Kalam's presidency immediately followed that of which President?
questions.explanation: He succeeded K.R. Narayanan, the tenth President.

questions.q: Kalam's presidency was immediately followed by that of which President?
questions.explanation: Pratibha Patil, the twelfth President, succeeded Kalam.

questions.q: The normal term of office of the President, which Kalam held, is how many years?
questions.explanation: The President is elected for a term of five years.

questions.q: The President whom Kalam became is a constituent part of which legislative body?
questions.explanation: Parliament consists of the President and the two Houses.

questions.q: Kalam's career path to the presidency is notable because he came chiefly from which background?
options: A scientific career rather than electoral politics
questions.explanation: He reached the presidency primarily from a scientific career rather than electoral politics.

questions.q: The space and missile programmes associated with Kalam fall under which broad national endeavour?
questions.explanation: His work belonged to India's science and technology development.

questions.q: Kalam's term as President fell across which span of years?
questions.explanation: His 2002 to 2007 term spanned the early-to-mid 2000s.

questions.q: As President, Kalam exercised the function of giving assent to which kind of measure passed by Parliament?
questions.explanation: The President gives assent to bills passed by Parliament so that they become law.

questions.q: Which sequence correctly reflects Kalam's path as shown on his card?
questions.explanation: He was an aerospace scientist before becoming President.

questions.q: The President's office held by Kalam is also described as belonging to which 'citizen' of India?
questions.explanation: The President of India is described as the first citizen of the country.

questions.q: The executive power of the Union, exercisable through the President whom Kalam became, is vested by which Article?
questions.explanation: Article 53 vests the executive power of the Union in the President.

questions.q: The President's power to grant pardons and commute sentences, available to Kalam in office, is contained in which Article?
questions.explanation: Article 72 gives the President the power to grant pardons, reprieves, respites and remissions.

questions.q: The office of President that Kalam held is itself established by which Article of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: Article 52 states that there shall be a President of India.

questions.q: The President, the office Kalam held, acts on the aid and advice of the Council of Ministers under which Article?
questions.explanation: Article 74 provides for a Council of Ministers to aid and advise the President.

questions.q: When Parliament is not in session, the President, as Kalam was, may promulgate ordinances under which Article?
questions.explanation: Article 123 empowers the President to promulgate ordinances when Parliament is not in session.

questions.q: Kalam was succeeded by Pratibha Patil, who is notable as which kind of President?
options: The first woman President of India
questions.explanation: Pratibha Patil, who succeeded Kalam, was the first woman President of India.

questions.q: Which statement about Kalam's route to the presidency is accurate?
options: He had not previously held the office of Vice-President
He served as Vice-President before becoming President
He was Speaker before becoming President
He was Prime Minister before becoming President
questions.explanation: Unlike several predecessors, Kalam came to the presidency from a scientific career and had not been Vice-President.

questions.q: The President's term of office, as held by Kalam, runs for five years from which point under the Constitution?
options: The date the President enters upon the office
questions.explanation: The five-year term is counted from the date the President enters upon the office.
